title: Implement cloud burst dispatcher: Go side that provisions RunPod pod
completion_summary: burst/ Go service at repo root with cobra CLI. internal/plan builds a 6-step shell pipeline (runpodctl create -> rsync trainer code -> rsync dataset -> ssh exec [uv sync + uv pip install unsloth + apprentice-train + apprentice-merge] -> rsync merged model back -> runpodctl remove). The training step is one bash -lc invocation guarded by `set -euo pipefail` so any sub-step's failure aborts before we pay for further pod minutes. internal/runner executes plan.Command lists with --dry-run support; AllowFailure commands (like terminate) log a warning but don't abort, so a cleanup hiccup never voids a successful training result. CLI: `burst plan` prints the full command sequence with no execution (CI-testable); `burst run --dry-run` does the same; `burst run` (real) requires runpodctl + rsync + ssh on PATH. Pod-ID parsing from runpodctl create's output is deliberately NOT implemented -- the format has drifted in the past, so the operator pattern is provision once in plan mode to grab the runpodctl line, run it manually, capture pod ID + host + port, then re-run with --skip-provision + --pod-id/host/port. 11 unit tests pass (8 plan + 3 runner): default pod fields, override propagation, runpodctl arg shape, rsync direction + ssh -p/-i flags, SSHExec script quoting, full Build() step ordering ends with AllowFailure-terminate, training script includes profile env + apprentice-train + apprentice-merge invocations, runner dry-run prints all, AllowFailure tolerates non-zero, non-AllowFailure aborts. Live RunPod execution acceptance bullet deferred to manual smoke run (needs RunPod account + cost). Deliverables: burst/{main.go, cmd/{root,plan,run,version}.go, internal/plan/{plan,plan_test}.go, internal/runner/{runner,runner_test}.go, go.mod}.
acceptance_criteria: Rsyncs dataset to pod, triggers training, rsyncs model back
`./apprentice train --mode=burst` provisions pod and returns merged model path
